title: Executive Order 12811—Waiver Under the Trade Act of 1974 With Respect to Tajikistan and Turkmenistan

flags.justification: Exercises a specific, narrow, congressionally-provided waiver mechanism (Trade Act §402(c)(2)), consistent with EO 12051 and EO 12740.
The order confirms the congressionally required precondition report was already made.
Single, narrow purpose covering two related newly-independent states.
A narrow statutory waiver, not a compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
Jackson-Vanik-related waivers under this section are periodically renewed by design.
The required congressional reporting precondition adds rather than removes oversight.
No third-party compliance regime is created.
A routine, narrow, congressionally-anticipated waiver exercise, extended to newly-independent former Soviet states.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
